what_it_does: A design note arguing that ambiguous referents are the central problem in annotation or margin-mark workflows, and proposing that the system store verbatim target text and detect orphaned anchors.
observations: The title frames the everyday ambiguity of the word 'this' in reviewer comments.
It argues the brief's hierarchy/preview-outline gesture is secretly a disambiguation engine, not merely a convenience.
It proposes that every stored envelope keep the resolved span text verbatim, even if the composer UI omits it while composing.
It distinguishes 'redundant on my screen now' from 'redundant in the record'.
It proposes orphan/reanchor handling: when target text changes under a live mark, the mark surfaces in a 'needs your eyes' lane.
It ties orphan detection to an existing content-signature freshness mechanism (design-map A7).
It references Tim's MSG2, BD-B ADDITIONS, BD-D ADDITIONS, and BD-G.
standouts: The reframing of hierarchy/preview-outline as a referent-disambiguation engine.
The requirement that stored envelopes carry verbatim anchored quotes of their targets.
The proposal for explicit orphan detection and reanchor surfacing in the overlay.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, channel, links, and history.
Cites design-brief sections (BD-B, BD-D, BD-G) and prior messages (Tim's MSG2) as evidence.
Follows an argumentative essay structure with a numbered section heading and bolded claims.
Uses bullet lists to present professional conventions.
concerns: Relies on several external artifacts (Tim's MSG2, BD-B/BD-D/BD-G, design-map A7) that are not included in the file.
The term 'envelope' is used without a file-local definition.
The 'agent' reader is assumed but not described.
notes: A design argument that the real value of the annotation hierarchy is referent disambiguation, backed by proposals for verbatim anchored quotes and orphaned-mark surfacing.
questions: What product or brief is this item responding to?
Who is Tim and what exactly did MSG2 say?
What are BD-B, BD-D, and BD-G in detail?
What is the precise definition of an 'envelope' in this system?
What is design-map A7 and how does the content-signature freshness mechanism work?
What does the 'needs your eyes' lane in the overlay currently look like?
How is the verbatim span text kept in sync with or distinguished from the live document?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the title and body both frame disambiguation of 'this' as the unnamed problem and propose concrete conventions to address it.
apparent_themes: Record fidelity versus transient UI redundancy
intention_signals: It is trying to elevate referent disambiguation from a convenience feature to the core promise of the annotation system, and to introduce anchored quotations and orphan/reanchor handling as professional conventions.
novelty: It reframes the existing hierarchy/preview-outline gesture as primarily a disambiguation engine, and argues for storing verbatim resolved span text in the envelope so marks remain interpretable even if the document shifts.
contribution: The file contributes the framing that annotation marks must unambiguously pin their referents, plus two concrete conventions: every mark carries a verbatim anchored quote, and the UI surfaces orphaned marks when their target text changes.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design item arguing that annotation marks must disambiguate their referents by storing verbatim target text and by surfacing orphaned anchors when underlying content changes.
intention_for_embedding: To persuade the design team to treat referent disambiguation as the core value of the annotation workflow and to adopt anchored-quotation and orphan-handling conventions.
